title: Auto Mechanic

rationales.taskAutomation: OBD-II diagnostics and AI-assisted repair-guide lookup automate a real share of diagnosis, but hands-on teardown, part replacement and road-testing stay manual.
rationales.toolMaturity: Dealer and franchise diagnostic platforms with AI fault-pattern matching are mature and standard equipment in most shops.
rationales.adoption: Diagnostic AI is mainstream in dealership and chain service centers; independent shops have adopted more unevenly.
rationales.agenticExposure: An agent can read codes, order parts and schedule the bay, but cannot pull an engine or verify a repair with a road test.

tasksAtRisk: OBD-II fault-code interpretation and repair-guide lookup
tasksGrowing: EV and hybrid drivetrain diagnosis and high-voltage safety work
Complex intermittent-fault troubleshooting AI code readers miss
Customer trust-building around AI-flagged repair recommendations
salarySource: BLS OES May 2023, SOC 49-3023, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Auto mechanics carry more exposure than most trades because AI-assisted diagnostic platforms are mature, mainstream in dealership service bays, and genuinely automate fault-code interpretation. The mixed trend reflects two forces pulling apart: EV and ADAS complexity is creating higher-skill demand even as routine ICE maintenance keeps shrinking with vehicle reliability and diagnostic automation.
